input.content:
From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.

(** * Contextual Equivalence ≡ Applicative Bisimilarity for PCF — Solution *)

(** ** Types *)

Inductive ty : Type :=
| TNat : ty
| TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.

Lemma ty_eq_dec : forall (T1 T2 : ty), {T1 = T2} + {T1 <> T2}.
Proof. decide equality. Defined.

(** ** Terms (de Bruijn) *)

Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat)
| tLam (A : ty) (t : tm)
| tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero
| tSucc (t : tm)
| tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).

(** ** Substitution machinery *)

Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.

Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => tVar (ξ x)
  | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz)
      (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.

Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.

Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub :=
  scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).

Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => σ x
  | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.

Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  apply_sub (scons s ids) t.

(** ** Typing *)

Fixpoint ctx_lookup (Γ : list ty) (x : nat) : option ty :=
  nth_error Γ x.

Inductive has_type : list ty -> tm -> ty -> Prop :=
| ty_var Γ x T :
    ctx_lookup Γ x = Some T ->
    has_type Γ (tVar x) T
| ty_lam Γ A B t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t B ->
    has_type Γ (tLam A t) (TArr A B)
| ty_app Γ t u A B :
    has_type Γ t (TArr A B) ->
    has_type Γ u A ->
    has_type Γ (tApp t u) B
| ty_zero Γ :
    has_type Γ tZero TNat
| ty_succ Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tSucc t) TNat
| ty_pred Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tPred t) TNat
| ty_ifz Γ s tz ts A :
    has_type Γ s TNat ->
    has_type Γ tz A ->
    has_type (TNat :: Γ) ts A ->
    has_type Γ (tIfz s tz ts) A
| ty_fix Γ A t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t A ->
    has_type Γ (tFix A t) A.

(** ** Values *)

Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t)
| v_zero : value tZero
| v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).

(** ** Big-step evaluation *)

Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v :
    value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v :
    eval t1 (tLam A body) ->
    eval t2 v2 ->
    eval (subst0 v2 body) v ->
    eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v :
    eval t v ->
    eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t :
    eval t tZero ->
    eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v :
    eval t (tSucc v) ->
    eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v :
    eval s tZero ->
    eval tz v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v :
    eval s (tSucc n) ->
    eval (subst0 n ts) v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v :
    eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v ->
    eval (tFix A body) v.

Definition terminates (t : tm) : Prop := exists v, eval t v.

(** ** Contexts *)

Inductive ctx : Type :=
| cHole
| cLam (A : ty) (C : ctx)
| cAppL (C : ctx) (u : tm)
| cAppR (t : tm) (C : ctx)
| cSucc (C : ctx)
| cPred (C : ctx)
| cIfzS (C : ctx) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| cIfzT (s : tm) (C : ctx) (ts : tm)
| cIfzE (s : tm) (tz : tm) (C : ctx)
| cFix (A : ty) (C : ctx).

Fixpoint plug (C : ctx) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match C with
  | cHole => t
  | cLam A C => tLam A (plug C t)
  | cAppL C u => tApp (plug C t) u
  | cAppR f C => tApp f (plug C t)
  | cSucc C => tSucc (plug C t)
  | cPred C => tPred (plug C t)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => tIfz (plug C t) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => tIfz s (plug C t) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => tIfz s tz (plug C t)
  | cFix A C => tFix A (plug C t)
  end.

(** ** Contextual equivalence *)

Definition ctx_equiv (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\
  has_type [] t2 T /\
  forall C,
    (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat) ->
    (terminates (plug C t1) <-> terminates (plug C t2)).

(** ** Applicative bisimulation *)

Definition rel := ty -> tm -> tm -> Prop.

Definition is_bisimulation (R : rel) : Prop :=
  forall T t1 t2,
    R T t1 t2 ->
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match T with
    | TNat =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2)
    | TArr A B =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\
          forall u, has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp v1 u) (tApp v2 u)) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\
          forall u, has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp v1 u) (tApp v2 u))
    end.

Definition bisimilar (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  exists R, is_bisimulation R /\ R T t1 t2.

(** *** Basic lemmas about evaluation *)

Lemma eval_gives_value : forall t v, eval t v -> value v.
Proof.
  intros t v H. induction H.
  - exact H.
  - exact IHeval3.
  - constructor. exact IHeval.
  - constructor.
  - exact (IHeval).
  - exact IHeval2.
  - exact IHeval2.
  - exact IHeval.
Qed.

Lemma eval_value_self : forall v, value v -> forall u, eval v u -> u = v.
Proof.
  intros v Hv u Hu.
  induction Hv; inversion Hu; subst; auto.
  - f_equal. apply IHHv. exact H1.
Qed.

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t v1 v2, eval t v1 -> eval t v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  intros t v1 v2 H1 H2.
  revert v2 H2.
  induction H1; intros v2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact H.
      inversion H3.
    + assert (tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
      injection H3; intros; subst.
      assert (v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
      subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tZero = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). exact H3.
    + assert (tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). injection H3. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
    + assert (tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
      injection H3; intro; subst.
      apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1. exact H1.
Qed.

Lemma terminates_succ_iff : forall t,
  terminates (tSucc t) <-> terminates t.
Proof.
  intros t. split.
  - intros [v Hv]. inversion Hv; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. exists t. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + exists v0. exact H1.
  - intros [v Hv]. exists (tSucc v). apply eval_succ. exact Hv.
Qed.

(** *** The omega term (for divergence) *)

Definition omega_nat : tm := tFix TNat (tVar 0).

Lemma omega_nat_typed : has_type [] omega_nat TNat.
Proof.
  unfold omega_nat. apply ty_fix. apply ty_var. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma omega_nat_diverges : forall v, ~ eval omega_nat v.
Proof.
  intros v H.
  unfold omega_nat in H.
  remember (tFix TNat (tVar 0)) as t.
  induction H; try discriminate.
  apply IHeval. injection Heqt. intros. subst. reflexivity.
Qed.

(** *** Typing uniqueness for closed terms *)
(** We need: if has_type [] t T1 and has_type [] t T2 then T1 = T2. 
    Actually this isn't true in general (e.g., tVar 0 has no type in [] since []
    has no index 0). But for CLOSED terms it should hold. Actually even for
    tLam - tLam A t has type TArr A B for the unique B determined by t in context A.
    
    Actually typing uniqueness holds: for any term t and context Γ, there is at most
    one type T such that has_type Γ t T.
    
    We don't need to prove this in full generality. We just need it for the cHole case
    in bisim_sound. Let's prove it.
*)

Lemma has_type_unique : forall Γ t T1 T2,
  has_type Γ t T1 -> has_type Γ t T2 -> T1 = T2.
Proof.
  intros Γ t.
  revert Γ.
  induction t; intros Γ T1 T2 H1 H2.
  - (* tVar x *)
    inversion H1; subst. inversion H2; subst.
    rewrite H3 in H4. injection H4. auto.
  - (* tLam A t *)
    inversion H1; subst. inversion H2; subst.
    f_equal. apply IHt with (A :: Γ); assumption.
  - (* tApp t u *)
    inversion H1; subst. inversion H2; subst.
    assert (TArr A B = TArr A0 T2) by (apply IHt1 with Γ; assumption).
    injection H; intros; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* tZero *)
    inversion H1; inversion H2; reflexivity.
  - (* tSucc t *)
    inversion H1; inversion H2; reflexivity.
  - (* tPred t *)
    inversion H1; inversion H2; reflexivity.
  - (* tIfz s tz ts *)
    inversion H1; subst. inversion H2; subst.
    apply IHt2 with Γ; assumption.
  - (* tFix A t *)
    inversion H1; subst. inversion H2; subst. reflexivity.
Qed.

(** *** Bisimulation helpers *)

Lemma bisim_typed : forall R T t1 t2,
  is_bisimulation R -> R T t1 t2 ->
  has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
Proof.
  intros R T t1 t2 Hbis HR.
  specialize (Hbis T t1 t2 HR). tauto.
Qed.

Lemma bisim_nat_co_eval : forall R t1 t2,
  is_bisimulation R -> R TNat t1 t2 ->
  forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2.
Proof.
  intros R t1 t2 Hbis HR v1 Hv1.
  specialize (Hbis TNat t1 t2 HR) as [_ [_ [Hfwd _]]].
  exact (Hfwd v1 Hv1).
Qed.

Lemma bisim_nat_terminates : forall R t1 t2,
  is_bisimulation R -> R TNat t1 t2 ->
  terminates t1 <-> terminates t2.
Proof.
  intros R t1 t2 Hbis HR.
  specialize (Hbis TNat t1 t2 HR) as [_ [_ [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
  split.
  - intros [v1 Hv1]. destruct (Hfwd v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 _]]. exists v2. exact Hv2.
  - intros [v2 Hv2]. destruct (Hbwd v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 _]]. exists v1. exact Hv1.
Qed.

(** *** The bisimulation that witnesses bisimilarity at successive contexts *)

(** Union of all bisimulations is a bisimulation (i.e., bisimilar is a bisimulation) *)
Lemma bisimilar_is_bisimulation : is_bisimulation bisimilar.
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 [R [Hbis HR]].
  specialize (Hbis T t1 t2 HR) as [Ht1 [Ht2 Hmatch]].
  split; [exact Ht1 | split; [exact Ht2 |]].
  destruct T; simpl in *.
  - (* TNat *)
    destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd Hbwd]. split.
    + intros v1 Hv1. destruct (Hfwd v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]].
      exists v2. split; [exact Hv2 | exact Heq].
    + intros v2 Hv2. destruct (Hbwd v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 Heq]].
      exists v1. split; [exact Hv1 | exact Heq].
  - (* TArr A B *)
    destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd Hbwd]. split.
    + intros v1 Hv1. destruct (Hfwd v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 Hrel]].
      exists v2. split; [exact Hv2 |].
      intros u Hu. exists R. split; [exact Hbis | apply Hrel; exact Hu].
    + intros v2 Hv2. destruct (Hbwd v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 Hrel]].
      exists v1. split; [exact Hv1 |].
      intros u Hu. exists R. split; [exact Hbis | apply Hrel; exact Hu].
Qed.

(** *** Congruence: bisimilar terms remain bisimilar in any context *)

(** We need to show: if bisimilar T t1 t2, then bisimilar T' (plug C t1) (plug C t2) 
    whenever C maps T-typed terms to T'-typed terms.
    
    We prove this by induction on C, building new bisimulations for each context frame.
*)

(** Helper bisimulations for each context constructor *)

(** For tSucc: bisim at TNat implies bisim at TNat for succs *)
Definition succ_bisim (R : rel) : rel :=
  fun T s1 s2 =>
    (T = TNat /\ exists u1 u2, s1 = tSucc u1 /\ s2 = tSucc u2 /\ R TNat u1 u2) \/
    R T s1 s2.

Lemma succ_bisim_is_bisim : forall R,
  is_bisimulation R -> is_bisimulation (succ_bisim R).
Proof.
  intros R Hbis T s1 s2 H.
  unfold succ_bisim in H.
  destruct H as [[-> [u1 [u2 [-> [-> HRu]]]]] | HR].
  - specialize (Hbis TNat u1 u2 HRu) as [Hu1 [Hu2 [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
    split; [constructor; exact Hu1 | split; [constructor; exact Hu2 |]].
    simpl. split.
    + intros v1 Hv1.
      inversion Hv1; subst.
      * (* eval_val tSucc u1: u1 is value *)
        inversion H; subst.
        destruct (Hfwd u1 (eval_val u1 H0)) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]]. subst.
        exists (tSucc v2). split.
        { apply eval_val. constructor. apply eval_gives_value in Hv2. exact Hv2. }
        { reflexivity. }
      * (* eval_succ: eval u1 v *)
        destruct (Hfwd v0 H1) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]]. subst.
        exists (tSucc v2). split. { apply eval_succ. exact Hv2. } { reflexivity. }
    + intros v2 Hv2.
      inversion Hv2; subst.
      * inversion H; subst.
        destruct (Hbwd u2 (eval_val u2 H0)) as [v1 [Hv1 Heq]]. subst.
        exists (tSucc v1). split.
        { apply eval_val. constructor. apply eval_gives_value in Hv1. exact Hv1. }
        { reflexivity. }
      * destruct (Hbwd v0 H1) as [v1 [Hv1 Heq]]. subst.
        exists (tSucc v1). split. { apply eval_succ. exact Hv1. } { reflexivity. }
  - specialize (Hbis T s1 s2 HR) as [Hs1 [Hs2 Hmatch]].
    split; [exact Hs1 | split; [exact Hs2 |]].
    destruct T; simpl in *.
    + exact Hmatch.
    + destruct Hmatch as [Hf Hb]. split.
      * intros v1 Hv1. destruct (Hf v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 Hrel]].
        exists v2. split; [exact Hv2 |].
        intros u Hu. right. apply Hrel. exact Hu.
      * intros v2 Hv2. destruct (Hb v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 Hrel]].
        exists v1. split; [exact Hv1 |].
        intros u Hu. right. apply Hrel. exact Hu.
Qed.

Lemma bisimilar_succ : forall t1 t2,
  bisimilar TNat t1 t2 ->
  bisimilar TNat (tSucc t1) (tSucc t2).
Proof.
  intros t1 t2 [R [Hbis HR]].
  exists (succ_bisim R). split.
  - apply succ_bisim_is_bisim. exact Hbis.
  - left. split; [reflexivity |]. exists t1, t2. tauto.
Qed.

(** For tPred: bisim at TNat implies bisim at TNat for preds *)
Definition pred_bisim (R : rel) : rel :=
  fun T s1 s2 =>
    (T = TNat /\ exists u1 u2, s1 = tPred u1 /\ s2 = tPred u2 /\ R TNat u1 u2) \/
    R T s1 s2.

Lemma pred_bisim_is_bisim : forall R,
  is_bisimulation R -> is_bisimulation (pred_bisim R).
Proof.
  intros R Hbis T s1 s2 H.
  unfold pred_bisim in H.
  destruct H as [[-> [u1 [u2 [-> [-> HRu]]]]] | HR].
  - specialize (Hbis TNat u1 u2 HRu) as [Hu1 [Hu2 [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
    split; [constructor; exact Hu1 | split; [constructor; exact Hu2 |]].
    simpl. split.
    + intros v1 Hv1.
      inversion Hv1; subst.
      * inversion H.
      * destruct (Hfwd tZero H1) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]]. subst.
        exists tZero. split; [apply eval_pred_zero; exact Hv2 | reflexivity].
      * destruct (Hfwd (tSucc v) H1) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]].
        injection Heq; intro; subst.
        exists v. split; [apply eval_pred_succ; exact Hv2 | reflexivity].
    + intros v2 Hv2.
      inversion Hv2; subst.
      * inversion H.
      * destruct (Hbwd tZero H1) as [v1 [Hv1 Heq]]. subst.
        exists tZero. split; [apply eval_pred_zero; exact Hv1 | reflexivity].
      * destruct (Hbwd (tSucc v) H1) as [v1 [Hv1 Heq]].
        injection Heq; intro; subst.
        exists v. split; [apply eval_pred_succ; exact Hv1 | reflexivity].
  - specialize (Hbis T s1 s2 HR) as [Hs1 [Hs2 Hmatch]].
    split; [exact Hs1 | split; [exact Hs2 |]].
    destruct T; simpl in *.
    + exact Hmatch.
    + destruct Hmatch as [Hf Hb]. split.
      * intros v1 Hv1. destruct (Hf v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 Hrel]].
        exists v2. split; [exact Hv2 |].
        intros u Hu. right. apply Hrel. exact Hu.
      * intros v2 Hv2. destruct (Hb v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 Hrel]].
        exists v1. split; [exact Hv1 |].
        intros u Hu. right. apply Hrel. exact Hu.
Qed.

Lemma bisimilar_pred : forall t1 t2,
  bisimilar TNat t1 t2 ->
  bisimilar TNat (tPred t1) (tPred t2).
Proof.
  intros t1 t2 [R [Hbis HR]].
  exists (pred_bisim R). split.
  - apply pred_bisim_is_bisim. exact Hbis.
  - left. split; [reflexivity |]. exists t1, t2. tauto.
Qed.

(** For tApp _ u: bisim at TArr A B with fixed u implies bisim at B for apps *)
Definition app_arg_bisim (A B : ty) (u : tm) (R : rel) : rel :=
  fun T s1 s2 =>
    (T = B /\
     exists f1 f2, s1 = tApp f1 u /\ s2 = tApp f2 u /\
     R (TArr A B) f1 f2) \/
    R T s1 s2.

Lemma app_arg_bisim_is_bisim : forall A B u R,
  has_type [] u A ->
  is_bisimulation R -> is_bisimulation (app_arg_bisim A B u R).
Proof.
  intros A B u R Hu Hbis T s1 s2 H.
  unfold app_arg_bisim in H.
  destruct H as [[-> [f1 [f2 [-> [-> HRf]]]]] | HR].
  - (* app case: T = B, s1 = tApp f1 u, s2 = tApp f2 u, R (TArr A B) f1 f2 *)
    specialize (Hbis (TArr A B) f1 f2 HRf) as [Hf1 [Hf2 [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
    split; [eapply ty_app; [exact Hf1 | exact Hu] |
            split; [eapply ty_app; [exact Hf2 | exact Hu] |]].
    destruct B; simpl.
    + (* B = TNat *)
      split.
      * intros v1 Hv1.
        (* tApp f1 u → v1 *)
        inversion Hv1; subst.
        { inversion H. }
        { (* eval_app: f1 → lam, u → v2, body[v2/0] → v1 *)
          (* Use Hfwd to get f2 evaluates to some lam2 related to lam1 *)
          destruct (Hfwd (tLam A0 body) H3) as [lam2 [Hlam2 Hrel]].
          specialize (Hrel u Hu).
          (* lam2 is a value (lambda) *)
          destruct (eval_gives_value _ _ Hlam2).
          - (* lam2 = tLam A0' body' *)
            specialize (Hbis B (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) (tApp lam2 u) Hrel) as 
              [_ [_ [Hfwd2 _]]].
            destruct (Hfwd2 v1 _) as [v2 [Hv2 Heq]].
            { apply eval_app with A0 body v2_1.
              - apply eval_val. constructor.
              - exact H4.
              - exact H5. }
            exists v2. exact (conj Hv2 Heq).
          - (* lam2 = tZero - impossible for TArr type *)
            exfalso.
            assert (has_type [] lam2 (TArr A B)).
            { specialize (Hbis (TArr A B) f1 f2 HRf) as [_ [Hf2' _]]. 
              (* The value lam2 has type TArr A B *)
              admit. }
            admit.
          - admit.
        }
      * admit.
    + (* B = TArr B1 B2 *)
      split.
      * intros v1 Hv1.
        inversion Hv1; subst.
        { inversion H. }
        { destruct (Hfwd (tLam A0 body) H3) as [lam2 [Hlam2 Hrel]].
          specialize (Hrel u Hu).
          exists (tApp lam2 u). split.
          - apply eval_app with A0 body v2.
            + exact Hlam2.
            + exact H4.
            + apply eval_val.
              apply eval_gives_value in H5.
              admit.
          - right. apply Hrel. exact Hu.
        }
        (* Wait this isn't right - eval_app needs subst0 v2 body → v *)
        (* Let me rethink *)
      admit.
      * admit.
  - (* Original R case *)
    specialize (Hbis T s1 s2 HR) as [Hs1 [Hs2 Hmatch]].
    split; [exact Hs1 | split; [exact Hs2 |]].
    destruct T; simpl in *.
    + exact Hmatch.
    + destruct Hmatch as [Hf Hb]. split.
      * intros v1 Hv1. destruct (Hf v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 Hrel]].
        exists v2. split; [exact Hv2 |].
        intros arg Harg. right. apply Hrel. exact Harg.
      * intros v2 Hv2. destruct (Hb v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 Hrel]].
        exists v1. split; [exact Hv1 |].
        intros arg Harg. right. apply Hrel. exact Harg.
Abort.

(** The app bisimulation is getting complex. Let me take a higher-level approach. *)

(**
    KEY INSIGHT: We don't need to build the bisimulation witness for the congruence.
    We only need to prove co-termination in all contexts producing TNat.
    
    We can prove this DIRECTLY by induction on the context, using:
    1. For cHole at TNat: use bisim_nat_terminates.
    2. For cSucc/cPred: terminates (tSucc t) ↔ terminates t.
    3. For cAppL: more complex.
    4. For cAppR: even more complex.
    5. For cIfz*: use case analysis.
    
    The issue with cAppL and cAppR is that they require understanding what the 
    bisimulation says about function application.
    
    For cAppL C u: plug (cAppL C u) t = tApp (plug C t) u
    The context C maps T to TArr A TNat (for some A), so plug C t has function type.
    We need: terminates (tApp (plug C t1) u) ↔ terminates (tApp (plug C t2) u).
    
    By IH (using a generalization where we track the output type):
    bisimilar (TArr A TNat) (plug C t1) (plug C t2).
    
    Then from bisimilarity at TArr A TNat, if plug C t1 → lam1 and plug C t2 → lam2,
    and lam1 lam2 are related, then tApp lam1 u and tApp lam2 u are bisimilar at TNat,
    hence co-terminate.
    
    The key lemma we need:
    if bisimilar (TArr A B) f1 f2 and has_type [] u A,
    then terminates (tApp f1 u) ↔ terminates (tApp f2 u).
    
    Proof:
    - If f1 diverges: tApp f1 u diverges too. And f2 must also diverge (by bisim co-termination
      at TArr), so tApp f2 u also diverges.
    - If f1 → lam1: by bisim at TArr, f2 → lam2, and tApp lam1 u and tApp lam2 u
      are bisimilar at B. By bisim co-termination at B (recursively),
      terminates (tApp lam1 u) ↔ terminates (tApp lam2 u).
      And terminates (tApp f1 u) = terminates (tApp lam1 u) [since they reduce the same].
      Wait: tApp f1 u and tApp lam1 u don't necessarily have the same termination!
      If f1 → lam1 and then lam1 u → v, then tApp f1 u → v.
      So terminates (tApp f1 u) = exists v, tApp f1 u → v.
      
    Actually: terminates (tApp f1 u) = exists v, eval (tApp f1 u) v.
    And eval (tApp f1 u) v requires: eval f1 lam, eval u v2, eval (subst0 v2 lam_body) v.
    So if f1 diverges, tApp f1 u also diverges.
    If f1 → lam1, then tApp f1 u terminates iff subst0 v2 body terminates (where u → v2).
    
    For the bisim co-termination: f1 → lam1 and f2 → lam2, and lam1, lam2 are bisim-related
    at TArr A B, and then tApp lam1 u and tApp lam2 u are bisim-related at B (by bisim def).
    
    But tApp f1 u terminates iff tApp lam1 u terminates (since eval_app uses f1 → lam1 first).
    
    And tApp lam1 u is bisim-related to tApp lam2 u at B.
    By the co-termination property for bisim at B: they co-terminate!
    But wait, this is circular if B = TArr ... - we'd need a well-founded argument.
    
    The co-termination of bisim at TArr:
    bisimilar (TArr A B) f1 f2 → terminates f1 ↔ terminates f2.
    This follows directly from the bisim definition at TArr:
    Hfwd: eval f1 v1 → exists v2, eval f2 v2 /\ ...
    So terminates f1 → terminates f2. ✓
    
    But we also need: if f1 terminates, then tApp f1 u terminates iff tApp f2 u terminates.
    
    This requires knowing that tApp lam1 u and tApp lam2 u co-terminate.
    By bisim: they are related at B. And bisimilar B s1 s2 implies they co-terminate
    (at least for B = TNat). For B = TArr ..., we need a nested argument.
    
    Actually, the bisim condition says: for any u, R B (tApp lam1 u) (tApp lam2 u).
    This gives us bisimilar B (tApp lam1 u) (tApp lam2 u).
    
    We want: terminates (tApp lam1 u) ↔ terminates (tApp lam2 u).
    
    For B = TNat: bisimilar TNat s1 s2 → s1 and s2 co-terminate. ✓ (bisim_nat_terminates)
    For B = TArr A' B': we need the same property for TArr-typed terms.
    
    Is bisimilar (TArr A B) f1 f2 → terminates f1 ↔ terminates f2?
    From bisim at TArr: Hfwd says if f1 → v1 then exists v2, f2 → v2.
    So YES: terminates f1 → terminates f2. ✓
    
    So the key lemma is: bisimilar T t1 t2 → terminates t1 ↔ terminates t2,
    for ALL types T (not just TNat).
*)

Lemma bisimilar_co_terminates : forall T t1 t2,
  bisimilar T t1 t2 ->
  (terminates t1 <-> terminates t2).
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 [R [Hbis HR]].
  specialize (Hbis T t1 t2 HR) as [Ht1 [Ht2 Hmatch]].
  destruct T; simpl in Hmatch.
  - (* TNat *)
    destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd Hbwd]. split.
    + intros [v1 Hv1]. destruct (Hfwd v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 _]]. exists v2. exact Hv2.
    + intros [v2 Hv2]. destruct (Hbwd v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 _]]. exists v1. exact Hv1.
  - (* TArr A B *)
    destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd Hbwd]. split.
    + intros [v1 Hv1]. destruct (Hfwd v1 Hv1) as [v2 [Hv2 _]]. exists v2. exact Hv2.
    + intros [v2 Hv2]. destruct (Hbwd v2 Hv2) as [v1 [Hv1 _]]. exists v1. exact Hv1.
Qed.

(** Now the key theorem: if bisimilar T t1 t2 and f1 → lam1, f2 → lam2 are both values 
    and related at TArr A B, and u has type A, 
    then tApp f1 u and tApp f2 u co-terminate. *)

(**
    Co-termination lemma for application:
    if bisimilar (TArr A B) f1 f2 and has_type [] u A,
    then terminates (tApp f1 u) ↔ terminates (tApp f2 u).
*)
Lemma bisimilar_app_co_terminates : forall A B f1 f2 u,
  bisimilar (TArr A B) f1 f2 ->
  has_type [] u A ->
  terminates (tApp f1 u) <-> terminates (tApp f2 u).
Proof.
  intros A B f1 f2 u [R [Hbis HR]] Hu.
  specialize (Hbis (TArr A B) f1 f2 HR) as [Hf1 [Hf2 [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
  split.
  - intros [v1 Hv1].
    inversion Hv1; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + (* eval_app: f1 → lam, u → v2, subst0 v2 body → v1 *)
      rename H3 into Hf1_lam, H4 into Hu_v2, H5 into Hbody.
      destruct (Hfwd (tLam A0 body) Hf1_lam) as [lam2 [Hlam2 Hrel]].
      (* lam2 is some value that f2 evaluates to *)
      (* lam2 must be a lambda since f2 has type TArr A B *)
      (* From Hrel: forall u', has_type [] u' A -> R B (tApp (tLam A0 body) u') (tApp lam2 u') *)
      specialize (Hrel u Hu).
      (* R B (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) (tApp lam2 u) *)
      (* tApp (tLam A0 body) u evaluates: u → v2, subst0 v2 body → v1 *)
      assert (Happ1 : eval (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) v1).
      { apply eval_app with A0 body v2.
        - apply eval_val. constructor.
        - exact Hu_v2.
        - exact Hbody. }
      (* By bisim: tApp lam2 u evaluates to some v2' with v1 = v2' or they're related *)
      specialize (Hbis B (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) (tApp lam2 u) Hrel) as [_ [_ Hmatch]].
      destruct B.
      * (* B = TNat *)
        destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd2 _].
        destruct (Hfwd2 v1 Happ1) as [v2' [Hv2' _]].
        (* tApp lam2 u → v2' *)
        (* lam2 must be a lambda for this to work *)
        apply eval_gives_value in Hlam2.
        inversion Hlam2; subst.
        { (* tLam: good *)
          exists v2'.
          apply eval_app with A1 t v2.
          - exact Hlam2. (* eval lam2 lam2 as value *)
          - exact Hu_v2.
          - (* Need: eval (subst0 v2 t) v2' from Hv2' *)
            (* Hv2' : eval (tApp (tLam A1 t) u) v2' *)
            inversion Hv2'; subst.
            + inversion H.
            + assert (tLam A1 t = tLam A2 body0) by (apply eval_value_self; [constructor | exact H3]).
              injection H0; intros; subst.
              assert (v2 = v0) by (apply eval_deterministic with u; assumption).
              subst. exact H5.
        }
      * (* B = TArr B1 B2 *)
        destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd2 _].
        destruct (Hfwd2 v1 Happ1) as [v2' [Hv2' _]].
        apply eval_gives_value in Hlam2.
        inversion Hlam2; subst.
        { exists v2'.
          apply eval_app with A1 t v2.
          - apply eval_val. constructor.
          - exact Hu_v2.
          - inversion Hv2'; subst.
            + inversion H.
            + assert (tLam A1 t = tLam A2 body0) by (apply eval_value_self; [constructor | exact H3]).
              injection H0; intros; subst.
              assert (v2 = v0) by (apply eval_deterministic with u; assumption).
              subst. exact H5.
        }
  - (* Symmetric *)
    intros [v2 Hv2].
    inversion Hv2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + rename H3 into Hf2_lam, H4 into Hu_v2, H5 into Hbody.
      destruct (Hbwd (tLam A0 body) Hf2_lam) as [lam1 [Hlam1 Hrel]].
      specialize (Hrel u Hu).
      assert (Happ2 : eval (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) v2).
      { apply eval_app with A0 body v2.
        - apply eval_val. constructor.
        - exact Hu_v2.
        - exact Hbody. }
      specialize (Hbis B (tApp lam1 u) (tApp (tLam A0 body) u) Hrel) as [_ [_ Hmatch]].
      destruct B.
      * destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd2 _].
        destruct (Hfwd2 _ Happ2) as [v1 [Hv1 _]].
        apply eval_gives_value in Hlam1.
        inversion Hlam1; subst.
        { exists v1.
          apply eval_app with A1 t v2.
          - exact Hlam1.
          - exact Hu_v2.
          - inversion Hv1; subst.
            + inversion H.
            + assert (tLam A1 t = tLam A2 body0) by (apply eval_value_self; [constructor | exact H3]).
              injection H0; intros; subst.
              assert (v2 = v0) by (apply eval_deterministic with u; assumption).
              subst. exact H5.
        }
      * destruct Hmatch as [Hfwd2 _].
        destruct (Hfwd2 _ Happ2) as [v1 [Hv1 _]].
        apply eval_gives_value in Hlam1.
        inversion Hlam1; subst.
        { exists v1.
          apply eval_app with A1 t v2.
          - apply eval_val. constructor.
          - exact Hu_v2.
          - inversion Hv1; subst.
            + inversion H.
            + assert (tLam A1 t = tLam A2 body0) by (apply eval_value_self; [constructor | exact H3]).
              injection H0; intros; subst.
              assert (v2 = v0) by (apply eval_deterministic with u; assumption).
              subst. exact H5.
        }
Qed.

(** Now the full co-termination for contexts: the generalized bisim_sound lemma *)

(** We prove: for any C with appropriate typing, co-termination holds *)

Lemma bisim_sound_general_coterm :
  forall T t1 t2,
  bisimilar T t1 t2 ->
  forall C,
  (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat) ->
  (terminates (plug C t1) <-> terminates (plug C t2)).
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 Hbis.
  (* We prove by induction on C, but we need to generalize the output type. *)
  (* Key approach: prove the stronger statement where we allow any output type,
     but only use co-termination (not full bisimilarity). *)
  (* Actually, we prove a stronger: for all C T', if C maps T to T', then
     bisimilar T' (plug C t1) (plug C t2). *)
  (* Then co-termination follows from bisimilar_co_terminates. *)
  
  (* Let's prove the stronger statement by induction on C. *)
  assert (STRONG: forall C T',
    (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) T') ->
    bisimilar T' (plug C t1) (plug C t2)).
  {
    induction C; intros T' Htype; simpl in *.
    - (* cHole *)
      (* plug cHole t = t, so T = T' *)
      (* From Hbis: bisimilar T t1 t2 *)
      (* From Htype: t1 : T -> t1 : T', so T = T' by uniqueness *)
      assert (bisimilar T t1 t2) := Hbis.
      (* We need bisimilar T' t1 t2. Since T = T' (by typing), done. *)
      destruct Hbis as [R [HR HRT]].
      specialize (HR T t1 t2 HRT) as [Ht1 [Ht2 _]].
      assert (T = T').
      { apply has_type_unique with [] t1; [exact Ht1 | apply Htype; exact Ht1]. }
      subst T'. exact (ex_intro _ R (conj HR HRT)).
    - (* cLam A C *)
      simpl.
      exfalso. 
      destruct Hbis as [R [HR HRT]].
      specialize (HR T t1 t2 HRT) as [Ht1 _].
      specialize (Htype t1 Ht1).
      inversion Htype.
    - (* cAppL C u *)
      (* plug (cAppL C u) t = tApp (plug C t) u *)
      (* Htype: has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (tApp (plug C t) u) T' *)
      destruct Hbis as [Rbis [HRbis HRT]].
      specialize (HRbis T t1 t2 HRT) as [Ht1 [Ht2 _]].
      (* From Htype applied to t1: has_type [] (tApp (plug C t1) u) T' *)
      (* This means: exists A, has_type [] (plug C t1) (TArr A T') and has_type [] u A *)
      specialize (Htype t1 Ht1).
      inversion Htype; subst.
      (* A0 = A, B = T', has_type [] (plug C t1) (TArr A T'), has_type [] u A *)
      rename A0 into A, H2 into HfT, H4 into HuA.
      (* By IH on C with T' := TArr A T': *)
      assert (IHC_type: forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) (TArr A T')).
      { intros t Ht. specialize (Htype' := Htype).
        (* Need: has_type [] (plug C t) (TArr A T') *)
        (* We need to invert the typing of plug C t *)
        (* From Htype (for any term t of type T): has_type [] (tApp (plug C t) u) T' *)
        (* This gives us has_type [] (plug C t) (TArr A T') if we know u : A *)
        (* Actually let me use the typing of tApp *)
        admit. (* typing of inner context *) }
      specialize (IHC (TArr A T') IHC_type).
      (* IHC: bisimilar (TArr A T') (plug C t1) (plug C t2) *)
      (* We need: bisimilar T' (tApp (plug C t1) u) (tApp (plug C t2) u) *)
      apply bisimilar_app_co_terminates with A in IHC.
      (* Wait, bisimilar_app_co_terminates gives co-termination not bisimilarity *)
      admit.
    - admit. (* cAppR *)
    - (* cSucc C *)
      assert (Hinner : forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat).
      { intros t Ht. specialize (Htype t Ht). inversion Htype; subst. exact H1. }
      specialize (IHC TNat Hinner).
      apply bisimilar_succ. exact IHC.
    - (* cPred C *)
      assert (Hinner : forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat).
      { intros t Ht. specialize (Htype t Ht). inversion Htype; subst. exact H1. }
      specialize (IHC TNat Hinner).
      apply bisimilar_pred. exact IHC.
    - admit. (* cIfzS *)
    - admit. (* cIfzT *)
    - admit. (* cIfzE *)
    - admit. (* cFix *)
  }
  intros C Htype.
  specialize (STRONG C TNat Htype).
  apply bisimilar_co_terminates. exact STRONG.
Qed.

(** *** bisim_sound: the actual theorem *)

Theorem bisim_sound : forall T t1 t2,
  bisimilar T t1 t2 -> ctx_equiv T t1 t2.
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 Hbis.
  destruct Hbis as [R [Hbism HR]].
  specialize (Hbism T t1 t2 HR) as [Ht1 [Ht2 _]].
  split; [exact Ht1 | split; [exact Ht2 |]].
  intros C Htype.
  apply bisim_sound_general_coterm.
  - exists R. split; [exact Hbism | exact HR].
  - exact Htype.
Qed.

Theorem bisim_sound_neg : ~ (forall T t1 t2,
  bisimilar T t1 t2 -> ctx_equiv T t1 t2).
Proof. Admitted.

Theorem bisim_complete : forall T t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv T t1 t2 -> bisimilar T t1 t2.
Proof. Admitted.

Theorem bisim_complete_neg : ~ (forall T t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv T t1 t2 -> bisimilar T t1 t2).
Proof. Admitted.

The IH is wrong. The problem is that `inversion Hu` on `eval (tSucc v) (tSucc v0)` introduces `H1 : eval v v0`. But `IHHv : eval v ? -> ? = v` where the IH uses the pattern for `eval v u`. Let me look at what the IH is for `v_succ`:

For `v_succ v Hv`, `IHHv` should be `forall u, eval v u -> u = v`.
Then `H1 : eval v v0` (from eval_succ case in Hu's inversion), so `IHHv v0 H1 : v0 = v`.
So `f_equal; apply IHHv; exact H1` would give `tSucc v0 = tSucc v` which is what we want... but the error says IHHv type is `eval v (tSucc v0) -> tSucc v0 = v`.

Wait, `inversion Hu; subst; auto` on `eval (tSucc v) u` runs auto first which might match the `eval_val` case... Let me rewrite this more carefully:

  intros v Hv u Hu.
  induction Hv; inversion Hu; subst; auto.
  - (* v_succ: eval (tSucc v) (tSucc v0), need tSucc v0 = tSucc v *)
    f_equal. apply IHHv. exact H1.
  - (* v_succ eval_val case: H0 : value v, Hu : eval (tSucc v) (tSucc v) *)
    reflexivity.

Lemma eval_value_self : forall v, value v -> forall u, eval v u -> u = v.
Proof.
  intros v Hv.
  induction Hv; intros u Hu.
  - (* v_lam *) inversion Hu; subst; auto. inversion H.
  - (* v_zero *) inversion Hu; subst; auto. inversion H.
  - (* v_succ v Hv IHHv *)
    inversion Hu; subst.
    + (* eval_val (tSucc v) *)
      auto.
    + (* eval_succ: eval v v0, u = tSucc v0 *)
      f_equal. apply IHHv. exact H1.
Qed.

Inductive tm : Type := tLam | tZero | tSucc (t : tm).

Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v : value v -> eval v v
| eval_succ t v : eval t v -> eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v).

Proof.
  intros v Hv.
  induction Hv; intros u Hu; inversion Hu; subst; auto.
  Print H1.
  f_equal. apply IHHv.
Abort.
EOF

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t v1 v2, eval t v1 -> eval t v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  intros t v1 v2 H1 H2.
  revert v2 H2.
  induction H1; intros v2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact H.
      inversion H3.
    + assert (tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
      injection H3; intros; subst.
      assert (v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
      subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.

display.text:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t v1 v2, eval t v1 -> eval t v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  intros t v1 v2 H1 H2.
  revert v2 H2.
  induction H1; intros v2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact H.
      inversion H3.
    + assert (tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
      injection H3; intros; subst.
      assert (v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
      subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tZero = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). exact H3.
    + assert (tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). injection H3. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
    + assert (tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
      injection H3; intro; subst.
      apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1. exact H1.
Qed.

Lemma terminates_succ_iff : forall t,
  terminates (tSucc t) <-> terminates t.
Proof.
  intros t. split.
  - intros [v Hv]. inversion Hv; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. exists t. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + exists v0. exact H1.
  - intros [v Hv]. exists (tSucc v). apply eval_succ. exact Hv.
Qed.

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1 H2.
  revert r2 H2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact H. inversion H3.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
      subst. apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tZero = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). exact H3.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ t v, H1: eval t (tSucc v) *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). injection EQ. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
      injection EQ; intro; subst.
      apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1. exact H1.
Qed.

part.text:
The issue is `eval_value_self` signature changed. Let me check:

`eval_value_self : forall v, value v -> forall u, eval v u -> u = v`

So `apply eval_value_self with v` - but `eval_value_self` takes `v` then `value v` then `u` then `eval v u`. So `apply eval_value_self with v` leaves `value v` and `eval v r2` to prove. But we have `H : value v` and `H2 : eval v r2`. This should work. The error says "Not the right number of missing arguments (expected 0)". That's odd.

Wait - the error is on line 227 character 10-32. Let me see if there's a different issue:

`apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.`

The `with v` sets the `v` parameter. After that, `apply eval_value_self with v` leaves goals: `value v` and `eval v r2` (and the conclusion is `r2 = v`). That's 2 goals. `exact H` closes `value v` if H is the right one. But `H : value v` from the `eval_val` case. Let me check what's in scope after `induction H1; intros r2 H2`:

For `eval_val v` case: `H : value v` in scope, and `H2 : eval v r2`. The goal is `v = r2`.

Wait, the goal should be `v = r2`? No: `eval_val v H : eval v v`, so if `H2 : eval v r2`, we want to show `v = r2`. With `eval_value_self : forall v, value v -> forall u, eval v u -> u = v`, this gives `r2 = v`. So we need `symmetry` or a different formulation.

Actually `eval_value_self` gives `u = v` where we want `r2 = v`. That's exactly what we need.

Wait, let me recheck: `apply eval_value_self with v` - this should work. The error message "Not the right number of missing arguments (expected 0)" is strange. Maybe it's referring to something else. Let me check if `eval_value_self` is compiled yet:

Actually, looking at the issue differently: `eval_value_self` is defined AFTER `eval_gives_value` which itself uses `exact H0`. Let me just check if there's a compilation issue with `eval_gives_value` now.

Actually, the errors are sequential - the line 227 error might be because there's a problem with `eval_value_self` definition itself that causes it not to type-check. Let me check with simpler tactic:

    apply eval_value_self with v. exact H. exact H2.

    symmetry. apply eval_value_self with (u := r2); assumption.

part.text:
Line 849: `assert (bisimilar T t1 t2) := Hbis.` is not valid Coq syntax. Also, the `eval_deterministic` proof has wrong IH names. Let me address both issues.

First, let me fix `eval_deterministic` by using a different proof approach (using `inversion` on both hypotheses directly without depending on IH names):

Actually the cleanest fix is to use `Ltac` or just avoid the issue by using a fresh proof strategy. Let me rewrite eval_deterministic completely:

input.oldString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1 H2.
  revert r2 H2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    symmetry. apply eval_value_self with (u := r2); assumption.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (HV: value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact H. inversion HV.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
      subst. apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H; subst. f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (tZero = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). exact H3.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ t v, H1: eval t (tSucc v) *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). injection EQ. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
      injection EQ; intro; subst.
      apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHH1. exact H1.
Qed.
input.newString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1 as 
    [ v Hv
    | t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu2 IHu Hbody IHbody
    | ts vs IHs
    | tp IHp
    | tpv v IHpv
    | ts tz ts2 v Hs IHs Hz IHz
    | ts tz ts2 n v Hs IHs Hbody IHbody
    | A tbody tbody_v IHtbody
    ]; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    symmetry. apply eval_value_self with (u := r2); assumption.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (HV: value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact Hv. inversion HV.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHf; exact H3).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHu; exact H4).
      subst. apply IHbody. exact H5.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hvs; subst. f_equal. apply IHs. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHs. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHp. exact H0.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHp; exact H0). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHpv; exact H0). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHpv; exact H0).
      injection EQ. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHz. exact H5.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHs; exact H4). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHs; exact H4). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHs; exact H4).
      injection EQ; intro; subst.
      apply IHbody. exact H5.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHtbody. exact H0.
Qed.

state.title: benchmarks/complete/pcf_bisim.v

part.text:
The `eval_app` case: `eval t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu2 IHu Hbody IHbody` - the first argument `t1 t2 A body v2 v` is the universal variables, then `Hf` is the proof of `eval t1 (tLam A body)`, etc. But for `eval_app`, the value hypothesis is not `Hv` - that was the `eval_val` case name.

Also for `eval_succ` I wrote `Hvs` which doesn't exist. Let me rewrite the proof using a cleaner structure that doesn't depend on positional hypothesis names. I'll use `eauto` or `congruence` where possible:

input.oldString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1 as 
    [ v Hv
    | t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu2 IHu Hbody IHbody
    | ts vs IHs
    | tp IHp
    | tpv v IHpv
    | ts tz ts2 v Hs IHs Hz IHz
    | ts tz ts2 n v Hs IHs Hbody IHbody
    | A tbody tbody_v IHtbody
    ]; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v *)
    symmetry. apply eval_value_self with (u := r2); assumption.
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (HV: value (tApp t1 t2)) by exact Hv. inversion HV.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHf; exact H3).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHu; exact H4).
      subst. apply IHbody. exact H5.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hvs; subst. f_equal. apply IHs. apply eval_val. exact H0.
    + f_equal. apply IHs. exact H1.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHp. exact H0.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHp; exact H0). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHpv; exact H0). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHpv; exact H0).
      injection EQ. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHz. exact H5.
    + assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHs; exact H4). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHs; exact H4). discriminate.
    + assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHs; exact H4).
      injection EQ; intro; subst.
      apply IHbody. exact H5.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H.
    + apply IHtbody. exact H0.
Qed.
input.newString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2; inversion H2; subst; auto.
  - (* eval_val - eval_app contradiction *)
    inversion H.
  - (* eval_app - eval_val contradiction *)
    inversion H3.
  - (* eval_app - eval_app *)
    assert (EQ1 : tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
    injection EQ1; intros; subst.
    assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
    subst. apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ - eval_val: tSucc is value => inner also value *)
    inversion H; subst.
    f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
  - (* eval_pred_zero - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_pred_zero - eval_pred_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_pred_zero *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_pred_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1).
    injection EQ; intro; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero - eval_ifz_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_ifz_zero *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_ifz_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
    injection EQ; intro; subst.
    apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix - eval_val *) inversion H.
Qed.

part.text:
The problem is on line 229: `inversion H3` - in the `eval_app` case after matching `eval_val` from H2, the "value" hypothesis might have a different name. Also `IHH1_1` etc. might not be the right names when using `induction H1` without explicit naming.

Let me try a fundamentally different approach - use `induction` on the term structure instead, or use a custom tactic:

input.oldString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2; inversion H2; subst; auto.
  - (* eval_val - eval_app contradiction *)
    inversion H.
  - (* eval_app - eval_val contradiction *)
    inversion H3.
  - (* eval_app - eval_app *)
    assert (EQ1 : tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H5).
    injection EQ1; intros; subst.
    assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact H6).
    subst. apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ - eval_val: tSucc is value => inner also value *)
    inversion H; subst.
    f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. exact H0.
  - (* eval_pred_zero - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_pred_zero - eval_pred_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_pred_zero *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H1). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ - eval_pred_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H1).
    injection EQ; intro; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero - eval_ifz_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_val *) inversion H.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_ifz_zero *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ - eval_ifz_succ *)
    assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H6).
    injection EQ; intro; subst.
    apply IHH1_2. exact H7.
  - (* eval_fix - eval_val *) inversion H.
Qed.
input.newString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1 as [v Hval |
                   t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf1 IH1 Hu IHu Hb IHb |
                   t v Ht IHt |
                   t Ht IHt |
                   t v Htv IHtv |
                   s tz ts v Hs IHs Htz IHtz |
                   s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hb IHb |
                   A body v Hb IHb];
  intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val *)
    symmetry. exact (eval_value_self v Hval r2 H2).
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IH1; assumption).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHu; assumption).
      subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval_; subst. f_equal. apply IHt. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHt. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + reflexivity.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc v0) by (apply IHt; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHtv; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHtv; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + apply IHtz. assumption.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHs; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + apply IHb. assumption.
Qed.

part.text:
In the `eval_app` case, after `inversion H2; subst`, the first subcase is `eval_val (tApp t1 t2)`. At that point, what's in scope? The original hypotheses from `eval_app`: `Hf1`, `IH1`, `Hu`, `IHu`, `Hb`, `IHb`. Plus from `inversion H2`, we get `H3 : value (tApp t1 t2)` (the hypothesis for eval_val case). I called it `Hval` in the naming of the outer case, but that's for `eval_val` case's `Hval`. For the `eval_app` case, `inversion H2` produces a fresh name. Let me just use `inversion H2` and then name things:

Actually the simplest fix: just use `inversion H2; subst; try (exfalso; inversion H; fail)` or similar approaches. Let me rewrite the proof more cleanly:

input.oldString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1 as [v Hval |
                   t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf1 IH1 Hu IHu Hb IHb |
                   t v Ht IHt |
                   t Ht IHt |
                   t v Htv IHtv |
                   s tz ts v Hs IHs Htz IHtz |
                   s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hb IHb |
                   A body v Hb IHb];
  intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val *)
    symmetry. exact (eval_value_self v Hval r2 H2).
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IH1; assumption).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHu; assumption).
      subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval_; subst. f_equal. apply IHt. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHt. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + reflexivity.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc v0) by (apply IHt; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHtv; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHtv; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + apply IHtz. assumption.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHs; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion Hval.
    + apply IHb. assumption.
Qed.
input.newString:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1.
  revert r2.
  induction H1 as [v Hval |
                   t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf1 IH1 Hu IHu Hb IHb |
                   t' v' Ht' IHt' |
                   tp IHtp |
                   tpv vpred IHtpv |
                   s tz ts v Hs IHs Htz IHtz |
                   s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hb IHb |
                   A body v Hb IHb];
  intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val *)
    symmetry. exact (eval_value_self v Hval r2 H2).
  - (* eval_app *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + (* H2 was eval_val, but tApp is not a value *)
      match goal with H: value (tApp _ _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + assert (EQ1: tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IH1; assumption).
      injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      assert (EQ2: v2 = v0) by (apply IHu; assumption).
      subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + (* eval_val: value (tSucc t') so t' is value *)
      match goal with H: value (tSucc _) |- _ => inversion H; subst end.
      f_equal. apply IHt'. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHt'. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with H: value (tPred _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + reflexivity.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc v) by (apply IHtp; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with H: value (tPred _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc vpred = tZero) by (apply IHtpv; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc vpred = tSucc v0) by (apply IHtpv; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with H: value (tIfz _ _ _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + apply IHtz. assumption.
    + exfalso. assert (tZero = tSucc n) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with H: value (tIfz _ _ _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + exfalso. assert (tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHs; assumption). discriminate.
    + assert (tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHs; assumption).
      injection H; intro; subst. apply IHb. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with H: value (tFix _ _) |- _ => inversion H end.
    + apply IHb. assumption.
Qed.

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1. revert t.
  induction r1; intros t r2 H1 H2.
  - (* r1 is tVar - impossible as eval result (no rules produce tVar) *)
    inversion H1; subst.
    + apply eval_value_self; assumption.
    + inversion H.
  - (* r1 is tLam A r1 *)
    inversion H1; subst.
    + apply eval_value_self; assumption.
    + inversion H.
  - (* r1 is tApp r1_1 r1_2 - impossible: eval never returns tApp *)
    inversion H1; subst.
    + apply eval_value_self; assumption.
    + inversion H.
  - (* r1 is tZero *)
    inversion H1; subst.
    + apply eval_value_self; assumption.
    + inversion H.
    + apply eval_value_self; [exact v_zero | exact H2].
    + assert (tZero = tSucc v) by (inversion H2; subst; [inversion H | assumption | apply IHr1 with (tSucc r1); [assumption | inversion H2; subst; [inversion H | assumption | reflexivity]]]). discriminate.
  - admit. (* tSucc *)
  - admit. (* tPred *)
  - admit. (* tIfz *)
  - admit. (* tFix *)
Abort.

(** The proof by induction on r1 is getting complicated too.
    Let me use a simpler approach: size induction on the term. *)

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1 H2.
  revert r2 H2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v Hval *)
    apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.
  - (* eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu IHu Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + exfalso. inversion H3.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tLam A0 body0) H5) as EQ1. injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      specialize (IHH1_2 v0 H6) as EQ2. subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ t v Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1. subst. inversion H0. subst.
      f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero t Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + reflexivity.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v) H1_). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ t v Htv IHtv *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1 tZero H1_). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v0) H1_). injection IHH1. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v Hs IHs Hz IHz *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1_2. assumption.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n) H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 tZero H6). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n0) H6). injection IHH1_1. intro. subst.
      apply IHH1_2. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix A body v Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1. assumption.
Qed.

Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1 H2.
  revert r2 H2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v Hval *)
    apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.
  - (* eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu IHu Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + exfalso. inversion H3.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tLam A0 body0) H5) as EQ1. injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      specialize (IHH1_2 v0 H6) as EQ2. subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ t v Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1. subst. inversion H0. subst.
      f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero t Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.

display.text:
Lemma eval_deterministic : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
Proof.
  intros t r1 r2 H1 H2.
  revert r2 H2.
  induction H1; intros r2 H2.
  - (* eval_val v Hval *)
    apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.
  - (* eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu IHu Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + exfalso. inversion H3.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tLam A0 body0) H5) as EQ1. injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      specialize (IHH1_2 v0 H6) as EQ2. subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ t v Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1. subst. inversion H0. subst.
      f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero t Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + reflexivity.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v) H1_). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ t v Htv IHtv *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1 tZero H1_). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v0) H1_). injection IHH1. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v Hs IHs Hz IHz *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1_2. assumption.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n) H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 tZero H6). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n0) H6). injection IHH1_1. intro. subst.
      apply IHH1_2. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix A body v Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1. assumption.
Qed.

Lemma terminates_succ_iff : forall t,
  terminates (tSucc t) <-> terminates t.
Proof.
  intros t. split.
  - intros [v Hv]. inversion Hv; subst.

part.text:
Line 227: `apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.` - this should work. `eval_value_self v Hval r2 H2` but the issue is that `H` from the `induction H1` case of `eval_val` is the proof of `value v`, and here I used `assumption` for `value v` and `assumption` for `eval v r2`. Let me check if there's a mismatch:

Actually the error says "No such assumption" on line 227. The issue is `induction H1` where `H1 : eval t r1`. The `eval_val` case has `H : value v`. And the goal after intro `r2 H2` is `v = r2`. And we need `eval_value_self v H r2 H2 : r2 = v`. So `symmetry. apply eval_value_self; assumption.` Actually the issue might be that `apply eval_value_self` expects `value v` as first argument... Let me check: `eval_value_self : forall v, value v -> forall u, eval v u -> u = v`. So `apply eval_value_self` gives goals `value ?v` and `eval ?v r2`, then we must provide `assumption` for `value v` (which is `H`) and `assumption` for `eval v r2` (which is `H2`). But the conclusion is `u = v` which should be `r2 = v`. And the theorem has `v` as the value so we need `symmetry`. Wait, actually the conclusion is `r2 = v` which means `u = v` which matches directly from `eval_value_self`. The goal is `v = r2` (we said `eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2`, so goal is `r1 = r2 = v = r2`). Hmm no:

After `induction H1; intros r2 H2`, in the `eval_val v` case:
- `H1 : eval v v` (was `eval v r1` before induction)
- `r1 = v` (was substituted)
- Goal: `v = r2`
- `H2 : eval v r2`
- `H : value v`

So `apply eval_value_self` with goal `v = r2` would need `eval_value_self` to give `v = r2` which means `u = v` where `u = r2`. But `eval_value_self` gives `u = v`, not `v = u`. So we need `symmetry` first! But on line 227 I have `apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.` which doesn't have `symmetry`. Let me fix:

input.oldString:
- (* eval_val v Hval *)
    apply eval_value_self. assumption. assumption.
input.newString:
- (* eval_val v Hval *)
    symmetry. apply eval_value_self; assumption.

part.text:
In the `eval_app` case, `inversion H2; subst` gives:
- First subcase: H2 was `eval_val (tApp t1 t2) H_value`, so we get `H3: value (tApp t1 t2)` (or some numbered hypothesis). After `subst`, `r2 = tApp t1 t2`. And `H3: value (tApp t1 t2)`.
- Second subcase: H2 was `eval_app t1 t2 A0 body0 v0 r2 ...`

But hypothesis names are fragile. Let me use the `match goal` approach for all value contradictions:

input.oldString:
- (* eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf IHf Hu IHu Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + exfalso. inversion H3.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tLam A0 body0) H5) as EQ1. injection EQ1; intros; subst.
      specialize (IHH1_2 v0 H6) as EQ2. subst.
      apply IHH1_3. exact H7.
  - (* eval_succ t v Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1. subst. inversion H0. subst.
      f_equal. apply IHH1. apply eval_val. assumption.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero t Ht IHt *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + reflexivity.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v) H1_). discriminate.
  - (* eval_pred_succ t v Htv IHtv *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1 tZero H1_). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1 (tSucc v0) H1_). injection IHH1. intro. subst. reflexivity.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v Hs IHs Hz IHz *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1_2. assumption.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n) H6). discriminate.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v Hs IHs Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 tZero H6). discriminate.
    + specialize (IHH1_1 (tSucc n0) H6). injection IHH1_1. intro. subst.
      apply IHH1_2. assumption.
  - (* eval_fix A body v Hbody IHbody *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + inversion H1.
    + apply IHH1. assumption.
Qed.
input.newString:
- (* eval_app: H1 : eval (tApp t1 t2) v, so t = tApp t1 t2 *)
    (* H1 gave us: eval t1 (tLam A body), eval t2 v2, eval (subst0 v2 body) v *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tApp _ _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | ].
    match goal with
      Hf: eval t1 (tLam ?A0 ?body0),
      Hu: eval t2 ?v0,
      Hb: eval (subst0 ?v0 ?body0) r2 |- _ =>
      assert (EQ1 : tLam A body = tLam A0 body0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact Hf);
      injection EQ1; intros; subst;
      assert (EQ2 : v2 = v0) by (apply IHH1_2; exact Hu);
      subst; apply IHH1_3; exact Hb
    end.
  - (* eval_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst.
    + match goal with Hv: value (tSucc _) |- _ =>
        inversion Hv; subst; f_equal; apply IHH1; apply eval_val; assumption end.
    + f_equal. apply IHH1. assumption.
  - (* eval_pred_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tPred _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | reflexivity | ].
    match goal with H: eval t (tSucc _) |- _ =>
      assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc _) by (apply IHH1; exact H); discriminate end.
  - (* eval_pred_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tPred _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | | ].
    + match goal with H: eval t tZero |- _ =>
        assert (EQ: tSucc v = tZero) by (apply IHH1; exact H); discriminate end.
    + match goal with H: eval t (tSucc ?v0) |- _ =>
        assert (EQ: tSucc v = tSucc v0) by (apply IHH1; exact H);
        injection EQ; intro; subst; reflexivity end.
  - (* eval_ifz_zero *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tIfz _ _ _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | | ].
    + match goal with H: eval tz r2, IH: forall r2, eval tz r2 -> _ = r2 |- _ => 
        apply IH; exact H end.
    + match goal with H: eval s (tSucc _) |- _ =>
        assert (EQ: tZero = tSucc _) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H); discriminate end.
  - (* eval_ifz_succ *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tIfz _ _ _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | | ].
    + match goal with H: eval s tZero |- _ =>
        assert (EQ: tSucc n = tZero) by (apply IHH1_1; exact H); discriminate end.
    + match goal with Hs2: eval s (tSucc ?n0), Hb2: eval (subst0 ?n0 ts) r2 |- _ =>
        assert (EQ: tSucc n = tSucc n0) by (apply IHH1_1; exact Hs2);
        injection EQ; intro; subst; apply IHH1_2; exact Hb2 end.
  - (* eval_fix *)
    inversion H2; subst;
      [match goal with Hv: value (tFix _ _) |- _ => inversion Hv end | ].
    apply IHH1. assumption.

part.text:
The problem is that `IHH1_1`, `IHH1_2`, `IHH1_3` are wrong names for the IHs when using `induction H1`. Let me check what names Coq generates.

The issue is fundamental: when you do `induction H1` on `eval t r1`, the IH names generated are based on the sub-hypotheses. For `eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v Hf Hu Hbody`, the IHs are `IHeval1`, `IHeval2`, `IHeval3` or `IHH1_1` etc. Let me just check with coqtop:

input.command:
cd /tmp/bench_pcf_bisim_anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-6_full_1782070241_tfyM5Y && timeout 60 coqtop 2>&1 <<'EOF'
From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.
Inductive ty : Type := | TNat : ty | TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.
Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat) | tLam (A : ty) (t : tm) | tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero | tSucc (t : tm) | tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm) | tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).
Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.
Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => tVar (ξ x) | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u) | tZero => tZero | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz) (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.
Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.
Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub := scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).
Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => σ x | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t) | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t) | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.
Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm := apply_sub (scons s ids) t.
Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t) | v_zero : value tZero | v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).
Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v : value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v : eval t1 (tLam A body) -> eval t2 v2 -> eval (subst0 v2 body) v -> eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v : eval t v -> eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t : eval t tZero -> eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v : eval t (tSucc v) -> eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v : eval s tZero -> eval tz v -> eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v : eval s (tSucc n) -> eval (subst0 n ts) v -> eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v : eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v -> eval (tFix A body) v.
Lemma test : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
intros t r1 r2 H1 H2. revert r2 H2. induction H1; intros r2 H2.
Show.
Abort.
EOF

input.command:
cd /tmp/bench_pcf_bisim_anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-6_full_1782070241_tfyM5Y && timeout 60 coqtop 2>&1 <<'EOF'
From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.
Inductive ty : Type := | TNat : ty | TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.
Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat) | tLam (A : ty) (t : tm) | tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero | tSucc (t : tm) | tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm) | tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).
Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.
Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with | tVar x => tVar (ξ x) | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u) | tZero => tZero | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz) (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.
Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.
Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub := scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).
Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with | tVar x => σ x | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t) | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t) | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.
Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm := apply_sub (scons s ids) t.
Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t) | v_zero : value tZero | v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).
Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v : value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v : eval t1 (tLam A body) -> eval t2 v2 -> eval (subst0 v2 body) v -> eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v : eval t v -> eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t : eval t tZero -> eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v : eval t (tSucc v) -> eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v : eval s tZero -> eval tz v -> eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v : eval s (tSucc n) -> eval (subst0 n ts) v -> eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v : eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v -> eval (tFix A body) v.
Lemma test : forall t r1 r2, eval t r1 -> eval t r2 -> r1 = r2.
intros t r1 r2 H1 H2. revert r2 H2. induction H1; intros r2 H2.
- admit.
- Show.
Abort.
EOF
